request.description: Opens GitHub authorization page. Run in browser, not Postman — returns a redirect.

Request a password reset email. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email is registered (to avoid account enumeration).

**Local dev note:** SMTP is not configured by default. The worker must be running (`uv run arq app.worker.WorkerSettings`) to process the job. To get the token without email, query the `password_reset_tokens` table directly:

```sql
SELECT encode(token_hash, 'hex') FROM password_reset_tokens ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;
```

Actually the token_hash is a SHA-256 hash — the plain token is only available at generation time. Run the worker and check its logs for the plain token, then set `reset_token` in your environment manually before calling Reset Password.

Reset password using the token received via email from POST /auth/forgot-password. Token expires in 1 hour and is single-use. Returns 204 No Content on success. All existing refresh tokens are revoked.

query.description: Opaque pagination cursor from next_cursor in the previous response. Omit for the first page.

request.description: Adds a user to the project with the given role. Enqueues a `send_project_invitation` email to the new member via ARQ. The worker must be running for the email to be delivered.

exec: r.forEach(s => { if (s.name === "Backlog") pm.environment.set("status_backlog_id", s.id); if (s.name === "In Progress") pm.environment.set("status_in_progress_id", s.id); if (s.name === "Done") pm.environment.set("status_done_id", s.id); });

request.description: Autocomplete search for project members by username prefix. Scoped to project membership — never leaks users outside the project. Returns up to 10 results with user_id, username, full_name, and avatar_url.

Creates a task in Backlog by default. If `assignee_ids` are provided, enqueues a `send_assignment_notification` email to each assignee except the creator. If `due_date` is set, the task is eligible for the hourly due-date reminder cron (fires 24h before due).

Creates a task with an explicit status. If `assignee_ids` are provided, enqueues a `send_assignment_notification` email to each assignee except the creator. If `due_date` is set, the task is eligible for the hourly due-date reminder cron (fires 24h before due).

query.description: Omit for first page. Auto-set from previous response.

request.description: Changes the task's status. Enqueues a `send_status_change_notification` email to all current assignees (except the user making the change) via ARQ.
Updates the task's assignee list. Enqueues a `send_assignment_notification` email to each newly added assignee (except the user making the change). Removing assignees does not trigger a notification.

query.description: Omit for first page. Auto-set from previous response.
Pagination cursor from previous response next_cursor

request.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of @mentions for the authenticated user, ordered newest first. Each item includes the source type (comment or task), task and project context, who mentioned you, and a body excerpt.

query.description: Required if the status has tasks. Provide the target status ID.
